does: This system card documents the safety architecture and mitigations built into GPT-5.1-CodexMax, a large language model specialized for code generation. The paper describes dual-layer safeguards operating at the model level—including adversarial safety training against harmful code generation, prompt injection attacks, and jailbreak attempts—and at the product level through deployment constraints like sandboxed execution environments, restricted network permissions, and configurable access controls. The work systematizes both the safety mechanisms themselves and the evaluation methodology used to validate their effectiveness against realistic threat scenarios.
connects: This work extends the growing literature on large language model safety cards and transparency documentation, building on frameworks established by prior work documenting safety measures in systems like GPT-4 and Claude. It responds to heightened concerns about code-generation models enabling malicious software development, prompt injection vulnerabilities in deployed systems, and the inadequacy of model-only safety measures without corresponding product-level constraints. The approach combines techniques from adversarial training, reinforcement learning from human feedback, and system-level access controls that have been explored separately in prior research on language model alignment and AI security.
enables: This work provides a template for transparent safety documentation in specialized AI systems and enables better informed deployment decisions for organizations using code-generation models in production. It points toward future research on measuring the actual effectiveness of layered safety mitigations in real-world settings, establishing benchmarks for evaluating code safety training, and developing better methods for ensuring safety properties persist under distribution shift or adversarial conditions. The systematic framing of model-level and product-level defenses could inform standards for evaluating and certifying dangerous-capability AI systems across other domains beyond code generation.
